skill.name: clinicaltrials-database
skill.description: Query ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for trial searches, NCT study details, status filters, locations, sponsors, pagination, and CSV exports.
skill.summary: Search and summarize ClinicalTrials.gov trial records through the public API v2.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are mostly false positives from Markdown backticks, API field names, and clinical phase terminology. The confirmed risks are limited to a Python helper script that calls the public ClinicalTrials.gov API and examples that write CSV exports to local files.

medium_findings.title: Network Requests to Public ClinicalTrials.gov API
medium_findings.description: The helper script and examples send user-selected search parameters to the public ClinicalTrials.gov API. This is expected behavior, but users should avoid placing private patient details in queries.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct requests.get calls target clinicaltrials.gov, and the skill purpose is API search. I found no evidence of credential collection or non-public third-party exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Local CSV Export Examples
low_findings.description: Documentation examples write API responses to local CSV files. The filenames are fixed examples and do not show path traversal or arbitrary file overwrite logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are explicit documentation examples for exports. They are local-only and do not execute code or transmit saved data elsewhere.

low_findings.title: Static Command and Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick and weak cryptography patterns map to Markdown code fences, inline API fields, dates, and clinical phase labels. No shell execution or cryptographic operation was found in those contexts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed lines contain Markdown fences, tables, endpoint text, and clinical terms. There is no Ruby code, shell command substitution, hashing, encryption, or signature verification logic.

content.value_statement: Clinical trial searches require precise filters across public registry data. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code query ClinicalTrials.gov and summarize trial records.
actual_capabilities: Search ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 by condition, intervention, location, sponsor, status, and NCT IDs.
Retrieve detailed study records for a specific NCT identifier.
Paginate through large search result sets using API page tokens.
Extract concise summaries with NCT ID, title, status, phase, enrollment, and last update date.
Request JSON or CSV responses for research workflows and spreadsheet analysis.
Use reference documentation for endpoints, parameters, response modules, and error handling.
limitations: The skill uses public ClinicalTrials.gov data and does not verify medical accuracy independently.
It is not a medical advice tool and cannot determine patient eligibility alone.
API rate limits apply, especially during bulk pagination workflows.
Trial records may be incomplete, outdated, or missing results sections.

use_cases.title: Find Recruiting Trials
use_cases.description: Search for active trials by condition, intervention, location, and recruitment status.

use_cases.title: Analyze Research Portfolios
use_cases.description: Track sponsors, phases, locations, and recent updates across a therapeutic area.

use_cases.title: Export Registry Data
use_cases.description: Collect trial records in JSON or CSV format for review, reporting, or spreadsheet analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Condition Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search ClinicalTrials.gov for recruiting trials for type 2 diabetes. Return NCT ID, title, status, phase, and location summary.

prompt_templates.title: Intervention and Phase Filter
prompt_templates.prompt: Find Phase 3 trials for pembrolizumab that are recruiting or active but not recruiting. Summarize sponsor, enrollment, and update date.

prompt_templates.title: Location-Based Matching
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for recruiting breast cancer trials near Boston, Massachusetts. Highlight eligibility notes, study contacts, and trial sites.

prompt_templates.title: Bulk Trial Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Collect all recruiting lung cancer trials, paginate safely, summarize phase distribution, top sponsors, and trials with available results.

output_examples.input: Find recruiting Alzheimer disease trials and summarize the first five results.
output_examples.output: A concise table-style summary with NCT IDs, trial titles, recruitment status, phase, enrollment counts, and last update dates.

output_examples.input: Look up NCT04852770 and extract key study details.
output_examples.output: A structured narrative covering title, sponsor, status, eligibility highlights, contacts, locations, interventions, and primary outcomes.

output_examples.input: Compare recent Phase 2 diabetes trials by sponsor and location.
output_examples.output: A comparison summary grouping trials by sponsor, geography, phase, recruitment status, and recent update activity.

best_practices: Use specific filters for condition, intervention, status, and location to reduce noisy results.
Limit page size or maximum results when exploring broad conditions for the first time.
Review eligibility criteria and official study contacts before using results for patient matching.
anti_patterns: Do not enter identifiable patient information into public API search queries.
Do not treat registry text as medical advice or final eligibility confirmation.
Do not run unbounded bulk searches without respecting API rate limits.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: No. It uses the public ClinicalTrials.gov API v2, which does not require authentication.

faq.question: Can it find trials by NCT ID?
faq.answer: Yes. The helper script includes a function for retrieving a detailed record by NCT identifier.

faq.question: Can it export results?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation shows CSV responses and local CSV export examples.

faq.question: Is it safe for patient matching?
faq.answer: Use it for research support only. Avoid identifiable patient data and confirm eligibility with study staff.

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What are the main security considerations?
faq.answer: It makes public network requests and can write CSV exports locally. No credential access or shell execution was found.
